steps: Go to https://login.bigcommerce.com and log in to your BigCommerce store
In the store control panel, navigate to Advanced Settings > API Accounts
Select 'Store-level API account' (for direct store access)
Enter a name for the account and configure the required OAuth scopes
Copy the Access Token (X-Auth-Token), Client ID, Client Secret, and note your Store Hash from the API Path shown
The store hash is the segment between /stores/ and /v3/ in the API Path (e.g., abc123xyz)

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Access Token (X-Auth-Token)
credential_fields.description: Your BigCommerce store API access token, sent as the X-Auth-Token header. Also note your store hash for constructing API URLs.

notes: The store hash is embedded in all API URLs: https://api.bigcommerce.com/stores/{store_hash}/v3/
Access token is shown only once at creation — copy it before leaving the page
Store-level API accounts are scoped to a single store; use app-level accounts for multi-store apps
BigCommerce requires a paid plan — no free tier for production stores
Scope permissions must be explicitly granted for each resource group

test_endpoint.description: List products to verify your API credentials and store hash are correct
